tasks.instruction: Przeczytaj tekst. Do każdej luki (1–6) dopasuj właściwe zdanie (A–H), aby powstał logiczny i spójny tekst. Dwa zdania zostały podane dodatkowo i nie pasują do żadnej luki.
tasks.text:
ENDANGERED SPECIES

Scientists estimate that the Earth is currently experiencing its sixth mass extinction event, with species disappearing at a rate estimated to be between 100 and 1,000 times higher than the natural background rate. 1. _________ Unlike previous mass extinctions caused by asteroid impacts or volcanic eruptions, this one has a single primary cause: human activity.

The main driver of species loss is habitat destruction. As forests are cleared for agriculture, wetlands are drained, and coastlines are developed, the natural environments on which wildlife depends are reduced to ever-smaller fragments. 2. _________ Many species simply cannot survive in such isolated, reduced habitats and eventually disappear.

Climate change is adding a new layer of threat. As temperatures rise and weather patterns shift, many species find that the environment they have adapted to over thousands of years is changing faster than they can evolve to cope. 3. _________ Species that depend on sea ice, such as polar bears and certain seal species, are particularly vulnerable.

Poaching and the illegal wildlife trade remain serious problems, especially for large, iconic animals. Despite international agreements, elephants are still killed for their ivory, rhinos for their horns, and tigers for their bones and skin. 4. _________ This creates a powerful financial incentive that is difficult to combat, particularly in regions where poverty is widespread.

Conservation efforts have achieved some notable successes. The mountain gorilla population has increased significantly in recent years thanks to intensive protection programmes in Rwanda, Uganda, and the Democratic Republic of Congo. 5. _________ Its recovery from near extinction in the twentieth century is considered one of conservation's greatest achievements.

Ultimately, protecting biodiversity requires more than saving individual species. It requires preserving entire ecosystems and changing the way humans use natural resources. 6. _________ Without nature's services, human societies themselves would struggle to survive.

options.text: Wildlife from isolated patches of habitat cannot move, find mates, or adapt to changing conditions.
Coral reefs are bleaching and dying as ocean temperatures rise, destroying habitats for thousands of marine species.
Illegal wildlife products can fetch extraordinary prices on the black market.
The bald eagle, the national symbol of the United States, was saved from extinction by banning the pesticide DDT.
Every species lost represents a permanent reduction in the richness and resilience of life on our planet.
Every ecosystem provides services that humans depend on, from clean water and air to pollination of crops.
Zoological parks and botanical gardens around the world maintain breeding programmes for hundreds of endangered species.
International conservation agreements have been signed by most of the world's governments, though enforcement remains a challenge.
